---
title: ""
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-section style="background-image: url('images/orbach-library.jpg')"}
::: {.hero-badge}
UC Riverside Library · Self-Paced Certificate
:::

# Graduate Research Core Competencies Certificate

Practical skills for every stage of your research journey — citation management, scholarly communication, data management, literature review, publishing, and more.

**Complete all modules at your own pace to earn your certificate.**
:::

## What You'll Learn

The **Graduate Research Core Competencies Certificate** is designed to support graduate students at all stages of their research journey by building practical, transferable skills essential to scholarly work.

Participants develop strategies for:

- **Effective citation management** using tools like Zotero and Paperpile
- **Advanced literature review techniques** including systematic and scoping reviews
- **Responsible research data management** and open data publishing
- **Scholarly communication** — ORCID, researcher identity, and open access
- **Publishing & sharing** theses, dissertations, and research articles
- **Graduation requirements** — formatting, ProQuest, and Graduate Division workflows

> The modules can be completed **in any order**, at your own pace. A certificate will be awarded upon completion of all modules.

---

```{=html}
<div class="canvas-reminder">
  <div class="canvas-reminder-body">
    <strong>Submit Assignments on Canvas to Earn Points</strong>
    <p>The knowledge checks on this website mirror the quizzes on Canvas and are for self-study practice. To receive credit toward your certificate, complete and submit all assignments through the <strong>Canvas course platform.</strong> Completing quizzes here does <em>not</em> count toward your grade.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Course Modules

```{=html}
<div class="module-grid">
<a class="module-card" href="modules/01-citation-management.qmd"><div class="module-number">Module 1</div><h3>Citation Management</h3><p>Use Zotero and Paperpile to organize your references and learn how to create biosketches for grant applications.</p><div class="module-footer"><span class="module-tag">Zotero</span><span class="module-tag">Paperpile</span><span class="module-tag">Biosketch</span></div><div class="module-cta">Start Module →</div></a>
<a class="module-card" href="modules/02-scholarly-communication.qmd"><div class="module-number">Module 2</div><h3>Scholarly Communication</h3><p>Build your academic identity with ORCID, explore open access publishing, and understand UC Open Access Policies and implementations.</p><div class="module-footer"><span class="module-tag">ORCID</span><span class="module-tag">Open Access</span><span class="module-tag">eScholarship</span></div><div class="module-cta">Start Module →</div></a>
<a class="module-card" href="modules/03-data-management.qmd"><div class="module-number">Module 3</div><h3>Data Management</h3><p>Create data management plans, organize your research data, and learn open data publishing with Dryad.</p><div class="module-footer"><span class="module-tag">DMP Tool</span><span class="module-tag">Dryad</span><span class="module-tag">FAIR Data</span></div><div class="module-cta">Start Module →</div></a>
<a class="module-card" href="modules/04-literature-review.qmd"><div class="module-number">Module 4</div><h3>Literature Review Strategies</h3><p>Design effective search strategies, understand types of reviews, and use UC Library resources.</p><div class="module-footer"><span class="module-tag">Database Search</span><span class="module-tag">Systematic Review</span><span class="module-tag">Annotated Bibliography</span></div><div class="module-cta">Start Module →</div></a>
<a class="module-card" href="modules/05-publish-share.qmd"><div class="module-number">Module 5</div><h3>Publish & Share Your Research</h3><p>Navigate copyright, fair use, open access options, and the path from dissertation to publication.</p><div class="module-footer"><span class="module-tag">Copyright</span><span class="module-tag">Fair Use</span><span class="module-tag">Open Access</span></div><div class="module-cta">Start Module →</div></a>
<a class="module-card" href="modules/06-graduate-academic-affairs.qmd"><div class="module-number">Module 6</div><h3>Graduate Academic Affairs</h3><p>Understand UCR formatting requirements, the ProQuest submission process, and graduation timelines.</p><div class="module-footer"><span class="module-tag">ProQuest</span><span class="module-tag">Formatting</span><span class="module-tag">Graduation</span></div><div class="module-cta">Start Module →</div></a>
<a class="module-card" href="modules/07-capstone.qmd"><div class="module-number">Module 7 · Capstone</div><h3>Research Plan</h3><p>Integrate all competencies into a comprehensive research plan — your capstone for the certificate.</p><div class="module-footer"><span class="module-tag">Capstone</span><span class="module-tag">Research Plan</span><span class="module-tag">🎓 Certificate</span></div><div class="module-cta">Start Module →</div></a>
</div>
```

---

## How to Earn Your Certificate

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}
### 📖 Step 1
**Complete each module** — read the content, explore resources, and watch the videos.
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
### ✏️ Step 2
**Pass the knowledge check** at the end of each module (where provided).
:::

::: {.column width="5%"}
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
### 🎓 Step 3
**Submit the Capstone Research Plan** to your instructor to receive your official certificate via Accredible.
:::

::::

---

## Get Help

Need additional support? Contact UCR Library experts:

| Topic | Contact |
|---|---|
| Citation Management | [libstemteam@ucr.edu](mailto:libstemteam@ucr.edu) or [Mary-Michelle Moore](mailto:marymichelle.moore@ucr.edu) |
| Scholarly Communication & Publish and Share Your Research | [Dr. Jing Han](mailto:jingh@ucr.edu) or [Michele Potter](mailto:michele.potter@ucr.edu)|
| Data Management & Data Services | [Dr. Barbara Martinez Neda](mailto:barbara.martinezneda@ucr.edu) |
| Literature Review | [UCR Library Teaching & Learning Services](https://library.ucr.edu/about/directory/staff) |
| Thesis/Dissertation Formatting | [UCR Graduate Division](https://graduate.ucr.edu/filing-resources) |

**UCR Library often offers synchronous workshops on the same topics. Check the [UCR Library website](https://library.ucr.edu) for upcoming sessions.**
